question: Design a `Book` class with private members `title`, `author`, and `isbn` (all strings). Provide public methods to set and get these values, ensuring basic encapsulation.

test_cases.input: Set title 'To Kill a Mockingbird', author 'Harper Lee', ISBN '978-0061120084'.
test_cases.expected:
Title: To Kill a Mockingbird
Author: Harper Lee
ISBN: 978-0061120084

errors: Attempting to access a private member ('title') directly from `main` (outside the class), which violates encapsulation and C++ access rules, leading to a compilation error.
